item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image is copyrighted by Ecuador 365 (see here) and the second image has not been found under any source as Creative Commons. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That origin story gave me a vague García Márquez feeling. Probably more thematic than in tone, but it did pop to mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Effing brilliant, but that raises the question of how she smokes without her lungs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really good. Weird, even a bit gory in a sense, without the trying too hard feeling that I feel often comes up with body horror attempts in fiction.

item.title: Visceral without being overdone
item.content:encoded:
It is hard to write up the more "medical" skips that deal with viscera and organs without it turning into bad comedy, but you did it very well. It's more sad than scary. I feel for this woman.

Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read the article. Weird, kinda creepy…

I read the joke at the end.

"What the FUCK?!?"

In case you didn't get it, I think the girl in that joke IS this old woman. The point being, there was prostitution for her to get the goods from the man, but in exchange for getting all the carpets, the man gave her not just one baby… many, many babies…

And that dead baby laughing creeped me out too. But couldn't the foundation get more cooperation by having darker-skinned doctors present?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: but pregnant ladies shouldn't smoke!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: VERY creepy SCP. +1 - but please, doc. As a tobacco smoker myself, give the old woman-thing her pipe! It's cruel not to!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I laughed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
congratulations. you have managed to make the single most disturbing SCP to imagine that i can recall having read.

seriously, try to imagine a video of that last interview

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The girl is pregnant.

item.title: Re: Let me try...
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I'd found that myself. I'm not sure if/how it applies, though.

item.title: Let me try...
item.content:encoded:
(Google)

Here we go; it's more commonly transliterated as "pishtaco", a legendary monster of the Andes which cuts people into pieces for various disgusting purposes. Some of which include eating their fat and selling their skin as chicharrones (deep fried pork rinds). Since the monster is usually depicted as a pale-skinned stranger, you can guess how popular white doctors are in that part of the Andes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gross. Seriously, just EWWWWWW!
Her psychology/reactions don't make a lot of sense, but that actually works because old people get weird like that.

For making me a little sick to my stomach, you get a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure how, but it manages to be weird and dull at the same time. The story at the end managed to be a somewhat interesting origin story but it just isn't enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's been a couple comments along these lines…added in a quick note with a little more detail on how this works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jesus christ. This is so fucking disturbing that I don't even know.

Minor gripe:

an indeterminate number of smaller embryos that somehow manufactures insulin

the pancreas analogue described here would also need to manufacture glucagon (a hormone which does the opposite of insulin) and, if its digestive system does anything, it'd need to secrete pancreatic juice into the small intestine analogue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it would more interesting at the beginning - a weird foray into teratological development. I think the lack of conservation of mass is where you lost me. I can deal with it when there's a reason, but that's too much magic in my science fiction for right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My god this is horrifying. My only complaint is that I Google'd "pishtaku" and came up with nothing relevant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Creepy.

Also, does the mass for the SCP-1709-01 instances come from her body, or does the mass appear from nowhere?

The mass comes from her body; she has several of these things developing in her chest at any one time. I declined to include the explication for length reasons.

What, the Foundation isn't willing to let an SCP have a few smokes in exchange for information/cooperation?

It's a combination of a) Dr. Iqbal being in my mind a compassionate human being who would actually have a problem giving someone smokes for their health's sake and b) kind of a ridiculous request; SCP-1709 doesn't really do anything except talk; she couldn't really make use of these items if they were granted to her.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's…not a bad idea at all. I may change it to reflect that. Thanks Khym.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Blah, misread what you were asking about the mass.

That's a good question.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh, i finally got the joke!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i dont understand the pun! wad he a leper? is it like keep the tip?
I'm being thick here, i know, but please enlighten me….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It works wonderfully!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish I could upvote more than once. I really like this, and everything about it.

"keep the change"

Is this an "I got you pregnant, keep the fetus" joke, or am I missing something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's creepy, almost squikky. That being said, execution was good. Even if the concept is… Almost dull.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh christ man. The eyes comment got me to imagine an old lady with fetus heads for eyes. Just… Fuck that man. I need to go die now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1709 lacks most of the major internal organs found in human specimens.

I assume she has normal eyes?

SCP-1709 communicates by somehow processing the remarks directed at it, and subsequently expectorating a human fetus that appears to be at approximately 10-12 weeks of development. This fetus (hereby designated as an instance of SCP-1709-01) will then vocalize a response to the inquiry or statement originally directed at SCP-1709.

Creepy.

Also, does the mass for the SCP-1709-01 instances come from her body, or does the mass appear from nowhere?

And you were informed at that time that your request has been denied based on the Foundation's prohibitions on providing addictive and harmful substances to subjects in our care

What, the Foundation isn't willing to let an SCP have a few smokes in exchange for information/cooperation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*coughs up half-formed fetus*

"Thanks a lot, soulless!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Instead, SCP-1709's vital biological functions are carried out by a series of organisms bearing a superficial resemblance to a mass of fused human fetuses in various stages of development.

Upboat.

Please have my babies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The "keep the change" part is supposed to be kind of a bad pun, but mostly it's supposed to be a disjointed version of what the skip considers humor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i liked this one, got my vote. unearthly

but can you tell me, i don think i understand the joke…is it a pun? or did i miss it alltogether?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let me see what I can do about it.

EDIT: Ugh, this is what I get for saying something's easy. It's proving kind of difficult to find something the right size without any goddamn watermarks on it. Anyone got a better picture offhand that could work?

All right, see if this works any better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this a lot, it's well written and it's wonderfully strange (a little bit generic in it's concept but the execution overcomes this with ease.)

Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The former is the one I mean, because it's just been resized to hell. The second is okay, really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you mean SCP-1709 or SCP-1709-01? Because both pictures are pretty easy to replace if need be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really, really like this, because it's so fucking weird. My only complaint is that the picture of SCP-1709 is too damned small and stretched out so much. It just looks really low quality for such a good article. It's not bad enough for me to cancel this upvote, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New SCP up. As always, feedback and comments welcomed.
